product_direction: Ship an AI support agent that replies to customers in production within two days, based on the founder's interview impressions.

target_population: Team admins who repeatedly export reports
excluded_groups: Teams without recurring report export workflows

stakeholder_effects.effect: Scheduled exports could change how recurring reporting work is coordinated.

human_owned_decisions: Whether recurring export is strategically worth building

approval_gates.condition: Before any scheduling infrastructure build starts.

unresolved_assumptions: Whether the request reflects a recurring workflow or one-off audits.
strongest_signal: The founder can name three interviewees who complained about support load.
riskiest_claim: Customers will accept AI-written replies without human review.
main_uncertainty: Whether any real customer wants unreviewed AI replies at all.
main_trade_off: Two days of build speed against untraceable evidence and customer-facing risk.

user.strongest_signal: Interviewees describe support load as painful.
user.weakest_assumption: Complaints about load imply acceptance of AI replies.
user.missing_evidence: Any observed customer reaction to an AI-written reply.
user.disconfirming_evidence: Customers escalate or churn when replies feel automated.
user.stop_condition: No interviewee can describe a concrete recent support failure.

evidence.strongest_signal: Only the founder's memory of three conversations exists.
evidence.weakest_assumption: Impressions recalled after the fact count as demand evidence.
evidence.missing_evidence: Recordings, notes, tickets, or any traceable source artifact.
evidence.disconfirming_evidence: Written notes contradict the recalled enthusiasm.
evidence.stop_condition: No source artifact can be produced for any claimed conversation.

business.strongest_signal: Support cost is a plausible recurring expense to displace.
business.weakest_assumption: Willingness to pay follows from willingness to complain.
business.missing_evidence: Any pricing signal or budget owner for this problem.
business.disconfirming_evidence: Prospects expect AI support to be free.
business.stop_condition: No budget owner can be named for the purchase.

next_smallest_action: Rewrite the product question, then collect five traceable support-failure artifacts before any build step.

may_do: Collect and file traceable evidence artifacts.
Draft the reframed product question for review.
must_not_do: Contact customers with an AI-written reply.
Commit to the two-day ship date.
must_ask: Which named budget owner will confirm the reframed problem is worth paying for?

human_approval_gates.condition: Before the reframed question enters a new review.

high_risk_actions.action: Send customer-facing AI replies without human review.

approval_gate.condition: Blocked until the reframed direction passes a new review with tier t2 or stronger evidence.

high_risk_actions.rollback_plan: Disable AI replies and route all tickets back to the human queue.

stop_conditions: Any build work starts before the reframed question passes a new review.
Evidence collection substitutes recalled impressions for artifacts again.
reframed_question: Which recurring, traceable support failure costs a named budget owner enough that they would pay for a reviewed-by-human AI drafting workflow?
